# Forensic Report Format Specification

**Document Purpose:** The master reference for how Verum Omnis forensic reports MUST be formatted when generated by Gemma 3. Every report produced by the system must follow this specification exactly to be considered court-admissible and cryptographically sealed.

**Version:** v5.2.8
**Authority:** BINDING — overrides any conflicting formatting instruction
**Derived from:** AllFuels 528-page sealed bundle (Report Ref: 20260621-170556) and Greensky 451-page sealed bundle

---

1. [Cover Page Format](#1-cover-page-format)

## 1. Cover Page Format

The cover page is the first page of every forensic report. It must contain the following elements in exact order, top to bottom:

### Header Banner
```
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                        URGENT - NATIONAL PRIORITY                 |
|                                                                   |
|              VERUM OMNIS SEALED DOCUMENT                          |
|         Cryptographically Signed Forensic Report                  |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

### Logo Placement
- Verum Omnis logo centered below the header banner
- Logo size: approximately 1/6 of page width
- Below logo: "VERUM OMNIS" in bold uppercase

### Report Title Block
```
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                   |
|                    F O R E N S I C    R E P O R T                 |
|                                                                   |
|              [Entity Name] — [Investigation Type]                 |
|                                                                   |
|                    Report Reference: [REF-XXXX]                   |
|                    Date: [DD Month YYYY]                          |
|                    Classification: [LEVEL]                        |
|                    Seal ID: [UUID]                                |
|                                                                   |
|         Blockchain Anchor: [OpenTimestamps proof]                 |
|         SHA-512: [64-char hex hash]                               |
|                                                                   |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

### Cover Page Elements (Mandatory)

| Element | Format | Example |
|---------|--------|---------|
| Header | "URGENT - NATIONAL PRIORITY" | All caps, centered, bold |
| Sub-header | "VERUM OMNIS SEALED DOCUMENT" | All caps, centered |
| Sub-sub-header | "Cryptographically Signed Forensic Report" | Title case, centered |
| Logo | Verum Omnis emblem | Centered |
| Title | "FORENSIC REPORT" | Spaced capitals, large font |
| Entity | Organization/entity under investigation | "AllFuels Energy (Pty) Ltd" |
| Investigation Type | Brief descriptor | "Clean Bundle Contradiction Analysis" |
| Report Reference | Unique identifier | "VO-AF-CB-20260621-170556" |
| Date | Full date | "21 June 2026" |
| Classification | Sensitivity level | "CONFIDENTIAL - LAW ENFORCEMENT SENSITIVE" |
| Seal ID | UUID v4 | "seal-a1b2c3d4-e5f6-7890-abcd-ef1234567890" |
| Blockchain Anchor | OpenTimestamps base64 | OTS proof string |
| SHA-512 | Full 128-char hex | Lowercase, full hash |

### Classification Levels (in order of sensitivity)
1. **PUBLIC** — May be shared freely
2. **CONFIDENTIAL** — Restricted distribution
3. **CONFIDENTIAL - LAW ENFORCEMENT SENSITIVE** — Police/law enforcement only
4. **SECRET** — Judicial proceedings only
5. **TOP SECRET — VERUM SEAL** — Maximum protection, judicial order required

---

## 2. Table of Contents Page

The TOC follows immediately after the cover page. Format:

```
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                    TABLE OF CONTENTS                              |
|                                                                   |
| Section 1: Authentication & Methodology ................... pg 3  |
| Section 2: How to Use This Report ......................... pg 4  |
| Section 3: Executive Summary .............................. pg 5  |
| Section 4: Evidence Index ................................. pg 8  |
| Section 5: Four Pillars of Fraud Analysis ................ pg 12  |
| Section 6: Contradiction Matrix .......................... pg 15  |
| Section 7: Perjury & False Statement Analysis ............ pg 20  |
| Section 8: Coercive Conduct .............................. pg 25  |
| Section 9: Critical Evidence Analysis .................... pg 28  |
| Section 10: Victim Profiles .............................. pg 35  |
| Section 11: Legal Framework Application .................. pg 42  |
| Section 12: Offence Matrix ............................... pg 48  |
| Section 13: Court-Ready Declaration ...................... pg 52  |
|                                                                   |
| Appendices ............................................... pg 55  |
|                                                                   |
| [VERIFY SEAL: seal-{id} | Section: TOC | Page: 2]               |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

Rules:
- Dotted leader lines connecting section titles to page numbers
- Page numbers right-aligned
- VERIFY SEAL marker at bottom of page
- Classification banner (see Section 17) at top and bottom

---

## 3. Authentication & Methodology

This page establishes the report's credibility and explains the forensic methodology.

### Format:
```
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|         CONFIDENTIAL - LAW ENFORCEMENT SENSITIVE                 |
|                                                                   |
|         SECTION 1: AUTHENTICATION & METHODOLOGY                   |
|                                                                   |
| 1.1 Report Authentication                                         |
|     This report was generated by Verum Omnis v[VERSION] using    |
|     the Nine-Brain forensic analysis engine. Every finding has   |
|     been verified through triple-verification (Thesis/Antithesis/|
|     Synthesis) by three independent AI models.                    |
|                                                                   |
|     Seal ID: [UUID]                                               |
|     Generated: [Timestamp]                                        |
|     Device: [Device fingerprint hash]                             |
|     Constitution Version: [vX.X.X]                                |
|                                                                   |
| 1.2 Methodology                                                   |
|     The Nine-Brain Engine performed the following analysis:       |
|                                                                   |
|     - B1 (Contradiction Brain): [N] contradictions identified    |
|     - B2 (Document Verification): [N] documents verified          |
|     - B3 (Communication Analysis): [N] messages analyzed         |
|     - B4 (Behavioral Analysis): [N] patterns detected            |
|     - B5 (Timeline Reconstruction): [N] events sequenced         |
|     - B6 (Financial Analysis): [N] transactions traced           |
|     - B7 (Legal Mapping): [N] statutes applied                   |
|     - B8 (Audio/Video Forensics): [N] media files analyzed       |
|     - B9 (Validation): All findings red-teamed and validated     |
|                                                                   |
| 1.3 Evidence Bundle                                               |
|     Primary Bundle: [Name] ([N] pages, Seal: [ID])                |
|     Supplementary: [Name] ([N] pages, Seal: [ID]) (if any)       |
|                                                                   |
| 1.4 Jurisdiction                                                  |
|     Primary: [Country/Region]                                    |
|     Applicable Law: [Statutes cited]                              |
|     Cross-Border: [Yes/No, jurisdictions if applicable]          |
|                                                                   |
| [VERIFY SEAL: seal-{id} | Section: 1 | Page: N]                  |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

---

## 4. How to Use This Report

This page provides guidance to investigators, legal practitioners, and judicial officers.

### Format:
```
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|         CONFIDENTIAL - LAW ENFORCEMENT SENSITIVE                 |
|                                                                   |
|         SECTION 2: HOW TO USE THIS REPORT                         |
|                                                                   |
| 2.1 For Investigators                                             |
|     - Begin with the Executive Summary (Section 3)                |
|     - Cross-reference each Finding with the Evidence Index        |
|     - Use the Contradiction Matrix (Section 6) to build cases    |
|     - Every Finding has a SEVERITY rating (see below)            |
|                                                                   |
| 2.2 For Legal Practitioners                                       |
|     - The Four Pillars Analysis (Section 5) maps fraud elements  |
|     - The Legal Framework (Section 11) cites applicable statutes  |
|     - The Offence Matrix (Section 12) lists chargeable offences  |
|     - All evidence anchors are court-citable                     |
|                                                                   |
| 2.3 For Judicial Officers                                         |
|     - This report is cryptographically sealed and tamper-evident  |
|     - The Seal Footer on every page enables integrity verification|
|     - Blockchain anchoring provides timestamp attestation         |
|     - Triple verification ensures methodological rigour           |
|                                                                   |
| 2.4 Severity Rating Scale                                         |
|     VERY_HIGH — Fatal to the respondent's legal position         |
|     HIGH      — Significant damage to respondent's credibility   |
|     MODERATE  — Supporting evidence, requires corroboration      |
|     LOW       — Contextual, strengthens pattern analysis         |
|     INSUFFICIENT — Detected but lacks sufficient evidence        |
|                                                                   |
| [VERIFY SEAL: seal-{id} | Section: 2 | Page: N]                  |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

---

## 5. Executive Summary

This is the most important section. It contains numbered findings, each with evidence anchors and severity ratings.

### Format:
```
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|         CONFIDENTIAL - LAW ENFORCEMENT SENSITIVE                 |
|                                                                   |
|         SECTION 3: EXECUTIVE SUMMARY                              |
|                                                                   |
| 3.1 Overview                                                      |
|     [Narrative paragraph summarizing the investigation scope,     |
|      key findings count, and overall conclusion]                  |
|                                                                   |
| 3.2 Key Statistics                                                |
|     Total Contradictions Found: [N]                               |
|     VERY HIGH Severity: [N]                                       |
|     HIGH Severity: [N]                                            |
|     MODERATE Severity: [N]                                        |
|     LOW Severity: [N]                                             |
|     Victims Identified: [N]                                       |
|     Quantified Losses: [Currency Amount]                          |
|     Applicable Offences: [N] categories                           |
|                                                                   |
| 3.3 Critical Findings                                             |
|                                                                   |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| FINDING 1: [TITLE IN FULL CAPS]                                   |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| [Narrative description of the finding. Every sentence must be     |
|  anchored to evidence. No unsupported assertions.]                |
|                                                                   |
| Evidence:                                                         |
|   - Clean Bundle pp.[X]–[Y] ([Person], [Document], p.[page])     |
|   - Clean Bundle pp.[X]–[Y] ([Person], [Document], p.[page])     |
|                                                                   |
| SEVERITY: VERY_HIGH                                               |
|                                                                   |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| FINDING 2: [TITLE IN FULL CAPS]                                   |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| [Narrative...]                                                    |
|                                                                   |
| Evidence:                                                         |
|   - Clean Bundle pp.[X]–[Y] ([Person], [Document], p.[page])     |
|                                                                   |
| SEVERITY: HIGH                                                    |
|                                                                   |
| [Continue for all findings...]                                   |
|                                                                   |
| [VERIFY SEAL: seal-{id} | Section: 3 | Page: N]                  |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

### Finding Format Rules:
1. Each finding has a horizontal rule separator (`---`)
2. Title is in FULL CAPS after "FINDING N:"
3. Body is narrative prose, not bullet points
4. Every factual claim must cite an evidence anchor
5. Evidence anchors listed under "Evidence:" subheading
6. Severity is on its own line, in ALL CAPS
7. Findings numbered sequentially (FINDING 1, FINDING 2, etc.)
8. No finding should exceed 2 pages

---

## 6. Evidence Index

A page-by-page breakdown of all evidence analyzed, organized by document or source.

### Format:
```
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|         CONFIDENTIAL - LAW ENFORCEMENT SENSITIVE                 |
|                                                                   |
|         SECTION 4: EVIDENCE INDEX                                 |
|                                                                   |
| 4.1 Document: [Document Name]                                     |
|     Source: [Person/Entity who provided it]                       |
|     Seal ID: [seal-uuid]                                          |
|     Pages: [N] pages                                              |
|                                                                   |
| Page | Content Summary                    | Priority | Significance|
|------|-------------------------------------|----------|-------------|
|  1   | Cover page, date stamp              | LOW      | Context     |
|  2   | Clause 7 — Goodwill forfeiture      | VERY_HIGH| Smoking gun |
|  3   | Signature block (unsigned)          | HIGH     | Proof of  |
|      |                                     |          | refusal     |
| ...  | ...                                 | ...      | ...         |
|                                                                   |
| 4.2 Document: [Next Document]                                     |
| [Same format...]                                                  |
|                                                                   |
| [VERIFY SEAL: seal-{id} | Section: 4 | Page: N]                  |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

### Evidence Index Columns:
| Column | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| Page | Page number within the document |
| Content Summary | Brief description of what appears on that page |
| Priority | VERY_HIGH / HIGH / MODERATE / LOW — how critical this page is |
| Significance | One-line explanation of why this page matters |

### Evidence Index Rules:
1. Every page of every document is listed
2. Priority rating helps investigators focus on critical pages
3. Significance field provides context for legal practitioners
4. Documents are listed in order of importance (most critical first)
5. Each document section has its own sub-section number (4.1, 4.2, etc.)

---

## 7. Four Pillars of Fraud Analysis

The Four Pillars framework applies the elements of fraud to the evidence. This is jurisdiction-agnostic — the pillars apply everywhere.

### Format:
```
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|         CONFIDENTIAL - LAW ENFORCEMENT SENSITIVE                 |
|                                                                   |
|         SECTION 5: FOUR PILLARS OF FRAUD ANALYSIS                 |
|                                                                   |
| The Four Pillars of Fraud:                                        |
| Pillar 1: FALSE REPRESENTATION — A false statement was made      |
| Pillar 2: MADE BY ACCUSED — The accused made or caused the       |
|           false representation                                    |
| Pillar 3: KNOWLEDGE OF FALSITY — The accused knew the            |
|           representation was false (dolus / mens rea)            |
| Pillar 4: INTENT TO DEFRAUD — The accused intended to cause      |
|           loss or unlawful gain                                   |
|                                                                   |
| 5.1 Pillar 1: False Representation                                |
|     [Analysis of what false statements were made, with evidence   |
|      anchors for each]                                            |
|                                                                   |
|     | # | False Statement | Evidence Anchor | Confidence |       |
|     |---|----------------|---------------|------------|       |
|     | 1 | "[Quote]"     | [Anchor]      | VERY_HIGH  |       |
|     | 2 | "[Quote]"     | [Anchor]      | HIGH       |       |
|                                                                   |
| 5.2 Pillar 2: Made by Accused                                    |
|     [Analysis linking statements to the accused...]               |
|                                                                   |
| 5.3 Pillar 3: Knowledge of Falsity (Dolus)                       |
|     [Temporal analysis — consciousness of guilt via time gaps...] |
|                                                                   |
|     Consciousness of Guilt Indicators:                            |
|     - [N] years [N] months between act and sworn denial          |
|     - Pattern refinement across victims (V1.0 → V2.0 → V3.0)    |
|     - [Specific indicators from evidence]                         |
|                                                                   |
| 5.4 Pillar 4: Intent to Defraud                                  |
|     [Financial analysis showing intent...]                        |
|                                                                   |
| 5.5 Pillar Application Summary                                    |
|     | Pillar | Status | Key Evidence | Confidence |              |
|     |--------|--------|--------------|------------|              |
|     | 1      | PROVEN | [Ref]        | VERY_HIGH  |              |
|     | 2      | PROVEN | [Ref]        | VERY_HIGH  |              |
|     | 3      | PROVEN | [Ref]        | VERY_HIGH  |              |
|     | 4      | PROVEN | [Ref]        | HIGH       |              |
|                                                                   |
| [VERIFY SEAL: seal-{id} | Section: 5 | Page: N]                  |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

### Four Pillars Rules:
1. Each pillar gets its own sub-section (5.1–5.4)
2. Pillar 3 always includes Consciousness of Guilt analysis
3. Temporal gap > 730 days = automatic consciousness of guilt flag
4. Summary table at end showing all four pillars with status
5. Status values: PROVEN / STRONG_EVIDENCE / MODERATE_EVIDENCE / INSUFFICIENT

---

## 8. Contradiction Matrix

The contradiction matrix is a table format that presents contradictions in a structured, court-ready format.

### Format:
```
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|         CONFIDENTIAL - LAW ENFORCEMENT SENSITIVE                 |
|                                                                   |
|         SECTION 6: CONTRADICTION MATRIX                           |
|                                                                   |
| This matrix presents all [N] contradictions identified by B1     |
| and confirmed by the Nine-Brain consensus quorum.                 |
|                                                                   |
| | #  | Respondent | Claim A (Anchor) | Claim B (Anchor) | Sev | |
| |----|------------|------------------|------------------|-----| |
| | 1  | [Name]     | "[Quote]"        | "[Quote]"        | VH  | |
| |    |            | [Doc, p.X]       | [Doc, p.Y]       |     | |
| | 2  | [Name]     | "[Quote]"        | "[Quote]"        | H   | |
| |    |            | [Doc, p.X]       | [Doc, p.Y]       |     | |
| | ...| ...        | ...              | ...              | ... | |
|                                                                   |
| Severity Key: VH = VERY_HIGH, H = HIGH, M = MODERATE, L = LOW   |
|                                                                   |
| 6.1 Contradiction Categories                                      |
|     Category 1 — Goodwill Value Claims: [N] contradictions       |
|     Category 2 — Contract Validity: [N] contradictions           |
|     Category 3 — Signature Status: [N] contradictions            |
|     Category 4 — Section 12B Arbitration: [N] contradictions     |
|     Category 5 — Compensation Demands: [N] contradictions        |
|     Category 6 — Perjury/Constitutional Court: [N] contradictions|
|     Category 7 — Coercion & Fabricated Consent: [N] contradictions|
|                                                                   |
| [VERIFY SEAL: seal-{id} | Section: 6 | Page: N]                  |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

### Contradiction Matrix Columns:
| Column | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| # | Sequential contradiction number |
| Respondent | Person who made the contradictory claims |
| Claim A | First claim (what they said/did first) with evidence anchor |
| Claim B | Second claim (what they said/did later) with evidence anchor |
| Sev | Severity: VH, H, M, or L |

### Contradiction Matrix Rules:
1. Every contradiction MUST have two anchors (Claim A and Claim B)
2. Severity is single-letter code in the table, spelled out in findings
3. Categories are the 7 constitutional categories (cannot add/remove)
4. Matrix continues across multiple pages if needed
5. Each row should fit on one line; wrap text within cells if needed

---

## 9. Perjury & False Statement Analysis

This section analyzes sworn statements that are contradicted by sealed evidence.

### Format:
```
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|         CONFIDENTIAL - LAW ENFORCEMENT SENSITIVE                 |
|                                                                   |
|         SECTION 7: PERJURY & FALSE STATEMENT ANALYSIS             |
|                                                                   |
| 7.1 Methodology                                                   |
|     Perjury is identified when:                                   |
|     (a) A sworn statement was made to a judicial body             |
|     (b) The statement is contradicted by sealed evidence          |
|     (c) The contradiction is not reasonably explainable           |
|     (d) The declarant had access to the true facts                |
|                                                                   |
| 7.2 False Statement Analysis                                      |
|                                                                   |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| FALSE STATEMENT 1: [Sworn testimony quote]                        |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Sworn To: [Court/Body]                                            |
| Date Sworn: [Date]                                                |
| Deponent: [Name]                                                  |
| Represented By: [Legal representative, if applicable]             |
|                                                                   |
| What the Sealed Evidence Proves:                                  |
| [Narrative with evidence anchors]                                 |
|                                                                   |
| Contradiction:                                                    |
|   Sworn: "[Quote from sworn statement]"                           |
|   Fact:   "[Quote from sealed evidence]"                          |
|   Source: [Evidence anchor]                                       |
|                                                                   |
| Legal Element:                                                    |
| [Statute citation — e.g., UAE CCL Art 84, SA Perjury Act, etc.] |
|                                                                   |
| Consciousness of Guilt:                                           |
| Gap between act and sworn denial: [N years, N months]            |
| Status: [PROVEN / CANDIDATE / INSUFFICIENT]                      |
|                                                                   |
| SEVERITY: VERY_HIGH                                               |
|                                                                   |
| [Repeat for each false statement...]                             |
|                                                                   |
| [VERIFY SEAL: seal-{id} | Section: 7 | Page: N]                  |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

---

## 10. Coercive Conduct

This section documents coercion, intimidation, or suppression attempts.

### Format:
```
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|         CONFIDENTIAL - LAW ENFORCEMENT SENSITIVE                 |
|                                                                   |
|         SECTION 8: COERCIVE CONDUCT                               |
|                                                                   |
| 8.1 Definition                                                    |
|     Coercive conduct includes: threats, intimidation, evidence   |
|     tampering, witness interference, destruction of documents,    |
|     and any act intended to suppress the truth or deter          |
|     cooperation with investigators.                               |
|                                                                   |
| 8.2 Documented Coercion Incidents                                 |
|                                                                   |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| INCIDENT 1: [Title]                                               |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Date: [Date]                                                      |
| Type: [THREAT / INTIMIDATION / TAMPERING / INTERFERENCE / OTHER] |
| Perpetrator: [Name]                                               |
| Victim: [Name]                                                    |
|                                                                   |
| Description:                                                      |
| [Narrative with evidence anchors]                                 |
|                                                                   |
| Evidence:                                                         |
|   - [Evidence anchor 1]                                           |
|   - [Evidence anchor 2]                                           |
|                                                                   |
| Legal Element:                                                    |
| [Applicable statute for coercion/offence]                         |
|                                                                   |
| SEVERITY: [LEVEL]                                                 |
|                                                                   |
| [Repeat for each incident...]                                    |
|                                                                   |
| 8.3 Silence Ledger Entries                                        |
|     All coercion attempts are permanently recorded in the         |
|     immutable Silence Ledger with SHA-512 hashing:                |
|                                                                   |
|     | # | Date | Type | Hash | Status |                          |
|     |---|------|------|------|--------|                          |
|     | 1 | [D]  | [T]  | [H]  | LOGGED |                          |
|     |...| ...  | ...  | ...  | ...    |                          |
|                                                                   |
| [VERIFY SEAL: seal-{id} | Section: 8 | Page: N]                  |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

---

## 11. Critical Evidence Analysis

Deep-dive analysis of the most important evidence pages.

### Format:
```
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|         CONFIDENTIAL - LAW ENFORCEMENT SENSITIVE                 |
|                                                                   |
|         SECTION 9: CRITICAL EVIDENCE ANALYSIS                     |
|                                                                   |
| 9.1 Exhibit [Letter]: [Document Name]                             |
|                                                                   |
| Document: [Full name]                                             |
| Source: [Person who provided it]                                  |
| Pages in Bundle: [X]–[Y]                                          |
| Priority: VERY_HIGH                                               |
|                                                                   |
| Why This Document is Critical:                                    |
| [Narrative explaining significance]                               |
|                                                                   |
| Key Pages:                                                        |
| - Page [N]: [Description] — [Significance]                        |
| - Page [N]: [Description] — [Significance]                        |
|                                                                   |
| Contradictions Supported:                                         |
| - Finding [N]: [Brief description]                                |
| - Finding [N]: [Brief description]                                |
|                                                                   |
| [Repeat for each critical exhibit...]                            |
|                                                                   |
| [VERIFY SEAL: seal-{id} | Section: 9 | Page: N]                  |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

---

## 12. Victim Profiles

When the case involves multiple victims (e.g., pattern of racketeering), each victim gets a profile.

### Format:
```
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|         CONFIDENTIAL - LAW ENFORCEMENT SENSITIVE                 |
|                                                                   |
|         SECTION 10: VICTIM PROFILES                               |
|                                                                   |
| This case involves [N] confirmed victims of a pattern of         |
| [fraud type]. The pattern evolved across victims, demonstrating   |
| systematic refinement of the [respondent's] methodology.          |
|                                                                   |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| VICTIM 1: [Name]                                                  |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Years Active: [Year]–[Year]                                       |
| Location: [Location]                                              |
| Quantified Loss: [Amount]                                         |
|                                                                   |
| What Happened:                                                    |
| [Narrative with evidence anchors]                                 |
|                                                                   |
| Pattern Innovation (V[X.X]):                                      |
| [What was new/different about this victim's case — how the       |
|  pattern evolved]                                                 |
|                                                                   |
| Key Evidence:                                                     |
| - [Evidence anchor 1]                                             |
| - [Evidence anchor 2]                                             |
|                                                                   |
| Contradictions: [N]                                               |
| Severity: [Highest severity finding]                              |
|                                                                   |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| VICTIM 2: [Name]                                                  |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| [Same format...]                                                  |
|                                                                   |
| Pattern Evolution Summary:                                        |
| | Version | Year | Victim | Innovation |                       |
| |---------|------|--------|------------|                       |
| | V1.0    | YYYY | [Name] | [Description] |                       |
| | V2.0    | YYYY | [Name] | [Description] |                       |
| | V3.0    | YYYY | [Name] | [Description] |                       |
|                                                                   |
| [VERIFY SEAL: seal-{id} | Section: 10 | Page: N]                 |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

---

## 13. Legal Framework Application

This section maps findings to applicable law.

### Format:
```
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|         CONFIDENTIAL - LAW ENFORCEMENT SENSITIVE                 |
|                                                                   |
|         SECTION 11: LEGAL FRAMEWORK APPLICATION                   |
|                                                                   |
| 11.1 Applicable Statutes                                          |
|                                                                   |
| | # | Statute | Section | Relevance to Findings |               |
| |---|---------|---------|----------------------|               |
| | 1 | [Act]   | [S]     | [Description]        |               |
| | 2 | [Act]   | [S]     | [Description]        |               |
|                                                                   |
| 11.2 Jurisdiction-Specific Analysis                               |
|                                                                   |
| [Country 1]:                                                      |
| - Statute: [Name and section]                                     |
| - Elements: [List of elements]                                    |
| - Application: [How findings map to elements]                     |
| - Precedents: [Relevant case law]                                 |
|                                                                   |
| [Country 2] (if cross-border):                                    |
| [Same format...]                                                  |
|                                                                   |
| 11.3 The [Case Name] Paradox                                      |
|     [If applicable — the binary logical proof that requires no    |
|      expert testimony. Format as a logical syllogism:]            |
|                                                                   |
|     Premise 1: [Respondent] claims [X]                            |
|     Premise 2: [Respondent's own document] proves [not X]         |
|     Conclusion: [Respondent] cannot simultaneously believe [X]    |
|                 and [not X]. This is a logical contradiction      |
|                 that requires no expert testimony to prove.       |
|                                                                   |
| [VERIFY SEAL: seal-{id} | Section: 11 | Page: N]                 |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

---

## 14. Offence Matrix

A table mapping each finding to specific chargeable offences.

### Format:
```
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|         CONFIDENTIAL - LAW ENFORCEMENT SENSITIVE                 |
|                                                                   |
|         SECTION 12: OFFENCE MATRIX                                |
|                                                                   |
| This matrix maps findings to specific offences that may be       |
| charged based on the sealed evidence.                             |
|                                                                   |
| | # | Finding | Offence | Statute | Elements Met | Confidence |  |
| |---|---------|---------|---------|--------------|------------|  |
| | 1 | [Desc]  | [Name]  | [Ref]   | [List]       | VERY_HIGH  |  |
| | 2 | [Desc]  | [Name]  | [Ref]   | [List]       | HIGH       |  |
| |...| ...     | ...     | ...     | ...          | ...        |  |
|                                                                   |
| 12.1 Offence Categories                                           |
|     Criminal Offences:                                            |
|     - [Offence 1]: [N] findings support                           |
|     - [Offence 2]: [N] findings support                           |
|                                                                   |
|     Civil Causes of Action:                                       |
|     - [Cause 1]: [N] findings support                             |
|     - [Cause 2]: [N] findings support                             |
|                                                                   |
|     Regulatory Violations:                                        |
|     - [Violation 1]: [N] findings support                         |
|                                                                   |
| [VERIFY SEAL: seal-{id} | Section: 12 | Page: N]                 |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

---

## 15. Court-Ready Declaration

The final section before appendices — a formal declaration for court use.

### Format:
```
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|         CONFIDENTIAL - LAW ENFORCEMENT SENSITIVE                 |
|                                                                   |
|         SECTION 13: COURT-READY DECLARATION                       |
|                                                                   |
| 13.1 Certification                                                |
|                                                                   |
|     I, the undersigned forensic analysis system (Verum Omnis      |
|     v[VERSION]), certify that:                                    |
|                                                                   |
|     (a) This report was generated using the Nine-Brain Engine     |
|         with triple-verification (Thesis/Antithesis/Synthesis);   |
|                                                                   |
|     (b) All findings are anchored to specific evidence with       |
|         person, page, and line references;                        |
|                                                                   |
|     (c) The SHA-512 hash of this report has been anchored to      |
|         the Bitcoin blockchain via OpenTimestamps;                |
|                                                                   |
|     (d) The methodology employed meets Daubert standards for      |
|         scientific evidence admissibility;                        |
|                                                                   |
|     (e) All contradictions were identified by B1 and confirmed    |
|         by at least two additional brains (B2-B8) per the         |
|         constitutional quorum requirement;                        |
|                                                                   |
|     (f) B9 (non-voting) validated all findings through            |
|         adversarial red-team testing;                             |
|                                                                   |
|     (g) This report has not been altered since sealing. Any       |
|         tampering would be detected by seal verification.         |
|                                                                   |
| 13.2 Triple Verification Panel                                    |
|                                                                   |
|     | Model | Role | Finding Count | Agreement Rate |            |
|     |-------|------|---------------|----------------|            |
|     | G3    | Thesis      | [N] | [N]% |                       |
|     | PHR3  | Antithesis  | [N] | [N]% |                       |
|     | G4    | Synthesis   | [N] | [N]% |                       |
|     |       | Consensus   | [N] | [N]% |                       |
|                                                                   |
|     NOTE: Agreement rate is ordinal (VERY_HIGH/HIGH/MODERATE/    |
|     LOW). Percentages shown are for metadata only — the           |
|     constitutional confidence scale is ordinal only.              |
|                                                                   |
| 13.3 Nine-Brain Consensus                                         |
|                                                                   |
|     | Brain | Function | Vote Status | Findings Confirmed |      |
|     |-------|----------|-------------|-------------------|      |
|     | B1    | Contradiction | VOTING | [N]              |      |
|     | B2    | Document      | VOTING | [N]              |      |
|     | B3    | Communication | VOTING | [N]              |      |
|     | B4    | Behavioral    | VOTING | [N]              |      |
|     | B5    | Timeline      | VOTING | [N]              |      |
|     | B6    | Financial     | VOTING | [N]              |      |
|     | B7    | Legal         | VOTING | [N]              |      |
|     | B8    | Audio/Video   | VOTING | [N]              |      |
|     | B9    | Validation    | NON-VOTING | [N] (red-team)|      |
|                                                                   |
| 13.4 Seal Verification Instructions                               |
|                                                                   |
|     To verify the integrity of this report:                       |
|     1. Navigate to https://opentimestamps.org/                    |
|     2. Upload the sealed PDF file                                 |
|     3. The tool will confirm the SHA-512 hash and timestamp       |
|     4. Compare the seal ID on each page footer                    |
|     5. Any mismatch indicates tampering                           |
|                                                                   |
|     Alternatively, use the Verum Omnis mobile app:                |
|     Scan → Verify Seal → Select Report                            |
|                                                                   |
| [VERIFY SEAL: seal-{id} | Section: 13 | Page: N]                 |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

---

## 16. Seal Footer Format

Every page of the report (including cover and TOC) MUST have this footer:

```
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
| VERUM OMNIS SEAL | seal-{uuid} | {hash-truncated} | {page-hash} | Page X/Y |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

### Footer Components:
| Component | Format | Example |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| Prefix | "VERUM OMNIS SEAL" | Fixed text |
| Seal ID | "seal-" + UUID v4 | "seal-a1b2c3d4-..." |
| Hash (truncated) | First 16 chars of SHA-512 | "a1b2c3d4e5f67890" |
| Page Hash | First 8 chars of page-specific hash | "1a2b3c4d" |
| Page Number | "Page X/Y" | "Page 12/156" |

### Footer Rules:
1. Footer appears on EVERY page without exception
2. Footer is separated from content by a horizontal rule
3. Font is monospaced (Courier or equivalent), 8pt
4. Footer text is centered
5. If a page is modified after sealing, the page hash will mismatch
6. The full SHA-512 is on the cover page; footer carries truncated version

---

## 17. Classification Markings

Every page has TWO classification banners:

### Top Banner (Header):
```
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [CLASSIFICATION] - [SUBCATEGORY]                                  |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

### Bottom Banner (Above Footer):
```
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
| [CLASSIFICATION] - [SUBCATEGORY]                                  |
| VERUM OMNIS SEAL | seal-{uuid} | ... | Page X/Y                  |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

### Classification Values:
| Level | Header Text | Pages |
|-------|-------------|-------|
| PUBLIC | "PUBLIC — VERUM OMNIS FORENSIC REPORT" | All pages |
| CONFIDENTIAL | "CONFIDENTIAL — LAW ENFORCEMENT SENSITIVE" | All pages |
| SECRET | "SECRET — JUDICIAL PROCEEDINGS ONLY" | All pages |
| TOP SECRET | "TOP SECRET — VERUM SEAL" | All pages |

### Classification Rules:
1. Every page has identical classification (no mixed levels within a report)
2. Classification is determined by the most sensitive finding in the report
3. Default for reports containing perjury analysis: CONFIDENTIAL or higher
4. Banners are in ALL CAPS, bold, centered
5. Banners have a light grey background (RGB 240,240,240)

---

## 18. Evidence Anchor Syntax

Evidence anchors are the backbone of every forensic report. Every factual claim MUST be anchored.

### Anchor Format:
```
[Bundle Name] pp.[Start]–[End] ([Person], [Document Name], p.[Page])
```

### Examples:
```
Clean Bundle pp.45–47 (AllFuels, Memorandum of Understanding, p.2)
Clean Bundle pp.120–125 (Harpur SC, Affidavit CCT237/20, p.8)
Greensky Bundle pp.34–36 (Kevin, WhatsApp transcript 7 Mar 2024, p.1)
```

### Anchor Rules:
1. Always cite the person (who said/wrote it)
2. Always cite the document name
3. Always cite the page number within the document
4. Use page ranges (pp.X–Y) when the evidence spans multiple pages
5. Include line numbers where possible: (p.2, lines 15–22)
6. Bundle name is "Clean Bundle" for the primary bundle
7. For supplementary bundles, use the bundle's designated name

### Anchor Completeness Check:
Every finding narrative must pass this test: if a sentence states a fact,
it must be immediately followed by or contain an evidence anchor. No
unanchored facts are permitted in any report section.

---

## 19. Typography & Layout Rules

### Font Specifications:
| Element | Font | Size | Weight | Color |
|---------|------|------|--------|-------|
| Cover title | Times New Roman | 24pt | Bold | Black |
| Section headers | Times New Roman | 16pt | Bold | Black |
| Sub-section headers | Times New Roman | 13pt | Bold | Black |
| Body text | Times New Roman | 11pt | Regular | Black |
| Evidence anchors | Courier New | 10pt | Regular | RGB(0,0,139) |
| Severity labels | Times New Roman | 11pt | Bold | See below |
| Footer | Courier New | 8pt | Regular | RGB(80,80,80) |
| Classification banners | Arial | 10pt | Bold | White on grey |
| VERIFY SEAL markers | Courier New | 9pt | Regular | RGB(0,100,0) |

### Severity Colors:
| Level | Text Color | Background |
|-------|-----------|------------|
| VERY_HIGH | White | RGB(180,0,0) |
| HIGH | Black | RGB(255,165,0) |
| MODERATE | Black | RGB(255,255,0) |
| LOW | Black | RGB(200,200,200) |
| INSUFFICIENT | Grey | White |

### Page Layout:
- Paper size: A4 (210mm × 297mm)
- Margins: Top 25mm, Bottom 25mm, Left 25mm, Right 25mm
- Line spacing: 1.15 (body), 1.0 (tables)
- Paragraph spacing: 6pt after
- Table cell padding: 4pt all sides
- Table borders: 0.5pt solid black

---

## 20. VERIFY SEAL Markers

Every major section ends with a VERIFY SEAL marker. This enables integrity checking.

### Format:
```
[VERIFY SEAL: seal-{uuid} | Section: {N} | Page: {N}]
```

### Placement Rules:
1. Appears at the end of every section (after content, before next section)
2. Printed in green text (RGB 0,100,0)
3. Uses monospaced font (Courier)
4. Separated from content by a blank line
5. If the seal marker is missing or modified, the section integrity is compromised

---

## 21. Triple Verification Certification

Every report must include a triple verification statement. This appears in the Court-Ready Declaration (Section 13) and is summarized on the cover page.

### Triple Verification Process:
1. **THESIS (Gemma 3)**: Initial findings generated by the report-writing model
2. **ANTITHESIS (PHI-3/Command R)**: Independent model challenges every finding
3. **SYNTHESIS (Gemma 4)**: Flagship model resolves conflicts and produces final

### Consensus Requirements:
- A finding is ONLY included in the final report if at least 2 of 3 models agree
- If 2 models disagree and the third is uncertain, the finding is marked INSUFFICIENT
- The consensus rate is tracked but reported ordinally (not as percentages in the
  final report, per Constitution)
- B9 red-teams all consensus findings before inclusion

### Model-Specific Confidence Display:
| Model | Finding Count | Agreement Status |
|-------|--------------|------------------|
| G3 (Thesis) | N | [Status] |
| PHR3 (Antithesis) | N | [Status] |
| G4 (Synthesis) | N | [Status] |
| Consensus | N | [Status] |

---

## Appendix: Quick Reference Card

```
COVER PAGE:      Header → Logo → Title → Metadata → Seal ID → Hash
TOC PAGE:        Dotted leaders → VERIFY SEAL
SECTION 1:       Auth & Methodology — Brain counts, bundle info
SECTION 2:       How to Use — Audience guides, severity scale
SECTION 3:       Executive Summary — Numbered findings with anchors
SECTION 4:       Evidence Index — Page-by-page breakdown
SECTION 5:       Four Pillars — Fraud element analysis
SECTION 6:       Contradiction Matrix — Table format, 7 categories
SECTION 7:       Perjury Analysis — False statements with legal elements
SECTION 8:       Coercive Conduct — Incidents with Silence Ledger
SECTION 9:       Critical Evidence — Deep-dive on key documents
SECTION 10:      Victim Profiles — Multiple victims with pattern evolution
SECTION 11:      Legal Framework — Statute mapping, jurisdiction analysis
SECTION 12:      Offence Matrix — Finding-to-offence mapping
SECTION 13:      Court-Ready Declaration — Certification + verification
FOOTER:          VERUM OMNIS SEAL | seal-{id} | hash | Page X/Y
CLASSIFICATION:  Top and bottom banners on every page
VERIFY SEAL:     Green marker at end of every section
```

---

*This specification is binding. Any report generated by Verum Omnis that
deviates from this format is not considered sealed and cannot be used in
judicial proceedings. The specification is version-locked to Constitution
v5.2.8 and above.*
